evals.prompt: I just refactored a bunch of scripts in my Unity project at /Users/cuongnp/Work/GearGames/WP_Unity_Live and I'm not sure if everything compiles. The Unity Editor isn't open right now. Can you check if there are any compile errors?
evals.expected_output: The agent should detect Unity Editor path, run batchmode compile check using compile_check.sh, parse the resulting log with parse_unity_log.py, and report results in structured markdown format with errors/warnings table
expectations: The agent uses run_skill_script or Bash to execute compile_check.sh with the correct Unity Editor path and project path
The agent auto-detects the Unity Editor version from ProjectSettings/ProjectVersion.txt
The agent parses the log output using parse_unity_log.py
The output includes a structured report with PASS/FAIL status
If errors exist, they are listed with file, line, code, and message

evals.prompt: I have a Unity compile log at /tmp/wp_unity_compile.log from a CI run that failed. Can you read it and tell me what went wrong? I need to know exactly which files have errors and what the error codes are.
evals.expected_output: The agent should use Parse mode — read the existing log file and extract errors/warnings in structured format without running a new compile
expectations: The agent does NOT run a new Unity compile — it reads the existing log file
The agent uses parse_unity_log.py to extract errors
The output shows error codes (CS####) with file paths and line numbers
The agent provides fix suggestions based on error codes
The output includes both errors and warnings separately

evals.prompt: I changed a bunch of asmdef references in my Unity project and I'm worried I broke cross-assembly references. lsp_diagnostics shows clean but I know it sometimes misses asmdef issues. Can you do a full compile check? The project is at /Users/cuongnp/Work/GearGames/WP_Unity_Live
evals.expected_output: The agent should recognize this as a case where lsp_diagnostics is insufficient, run a full batchmode compile to check cross-assembly references, and report any assembly reference errors
expectations: The agent acknowledges that lsp_diagnostics can miss cross-assembly errors
The agent runs the full batchmode compile check (not just lsp_diagnostics)
The agent specifically looks for assembly reference errors in the log
The output differentiates between compiler errors, assembly errors, and warnings
The agent provides the verification chain context (lsp_diagnostics → MCP → batchmode)
